revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''中村璋八'''（なかむら しょうはち、[[1926年]][[1月7日]]- ）は、中国哲学研究者、[[駒澤大学]]名誉教授。 神奈川...'
revision.text:
'''中村璋八'''（なかむら しょうはち、[[1926年]][[1月7日]]- ）は、中国哲学研究者、[[駒澤大学]]名誉教授。
神奈川県愛甲郡愛川町生まれ。1951年[[東京文理科大学]]文学科卒。1978年「[[五行大義]]の基礎的研究」で駒澤大学[[文学博士]]。[[東京都立町田高等学校]]教諭、駒澤大学文学部助教授、教授。<ref>『現代日本人名録』</ref>1996年定年退任、名誉教授。道教の[[陰陽五行説]]などを研究。
==著書== *『五行大義』明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1973 *『五行大義の基礎的研究』明徳出版社 1976 *『五行大義校註』汲古書院 1984 *『日本陰陽道書の研究』汲古書院 1985 *『[[菜根譚]]のことば』斯文会 My古典 2007
===共編著=== *『[[緯書]]の基礎的研究』[[安居香山]]共著 漢魏文化研究会 1966 のち国書刊行会 *『原典対照中国思想文学通史』編著 明治書院 1984 *『緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼』編 平河出版社 1993 *『選集道教と日本 第2巻 古代文化の展開と道教』[[野口鉄郎]]共編 雄山閣出版 1997 *『中国人と道教 林原フォーラム’97』編 汲古書院 1998
===訳注など=== *『緯書集成』全6巻 安居香山共編 漢魏文化研究室 1959-64 *『重修緯書集成』全6巻 安居香山共編 明徳出版社 1971-92 *『[[呂氏春秋]]』[[内野熊一郎]]共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1976 *『給食倫理』編著 第一出版 給食学双書 1977 *『[[食経]]』[[佐藤達全]]共編著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1978 *[[道元]]『作る心食べる心 [[典座教訓]]・赴粥飯法・正法眼蔵示庫院文』[[石山力山]]・[[中村信幸]]共訳著 第一出版 1980 『典座教訓・赴粥飯法』講談社学術文庫 1991 *『日本料理秘伝集成 原典現代語訳 第17巻 食品食物学・食医学』佐藤達全共編 同朋舎出版 1985 *『五行大義全釈』[[藤井友子]]共著 明治書院 1986 *[[洪自誠]]『菜根譚』石川力山共訳注 講談社学術文庫 1986 *『[[食物本草]]』佐藤達全共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1987 *[[都良香]]『都氏文集全釈』[[大塚雅司]]共著 汲古書院 1988 *蔡恒息『易のニューサイエンス [[八卦]]・[[太極図]]とコンピュータ』[[武田時昌]]共訳 東方書店 1989 *朱宗元,趙青樹『陰陽五行学説入門』[[中村敞子]]共訳 谷口書店 1990 *『[[周易]]本義』[[古藤友子]]共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書続編 1992 *洪丕謨,姜玉珍『中国算命術』中村敞子共訳 東方書店 1992 *『田氏家集全釈』[[島田伸一郎]]共著 汲古書院 1993 *『五行大義』[[清水浩子]]共著 明治書院 新編漢文選 1998 *『風俗通義』清水浩子共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書続編 2002 *洪応明『菜根譚 清朝本全訳』訳注 東方書店 2006 *『[[中右記]]部類紙背漢詩集』[[伊野弘子]]共訳注 汲古書院 2011
===記念論集=== *『東洋学論集 中村璋八博士古稀記念』汲古書院 1996
==注== <references />

'''中村 璋八'''（なかむら しょうはち、[[1926年]][[1月7日]]-[[2015年]][[6月2日]]<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html 中国哲学者の中村璋八さんが死去：朝日新聞]</ref>）は、中国哲学研究者、[[駒澤大学]]名誉教授。
神奈川県愛甲郡愛川町生まれ。1951年[[東京文理科大学]]文学科卒。1978年「[[五行大義]]の基礎的研究」で駒澤大学[[文学博士]]。[[東京都立町田高等学校]]教諭、駒澤大学文学部助教授、教授。<ref>『現代日本人名録』</ref>1996年定年退任、名誉教授。道教の[[陰陽五行説]]などを研究。

revision.comment: 「道教の陰陽五行説」はおかしい
revision.text:
'''中村 璋八'''（なかむら しょうはち、[[1926年]][[1月7日]] - [[2015年]][[6月2日]]<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html 中国哲学者の中村璋八さんが死去：朝日新聞]</ref>）は、中国哲学研究者、[[駒澤大学]]名誉教授。
神奈川県[[愛甲郡]][[愛川町]]生まれ。1951年[[東京文理科大学]]文学科卒。1978年「[[五行大義]]の基礎的研究」で駒澤大学[[文学博士]]。[[東京都立町田高等学校]]教諭、駒澤大学文学部助教授、教授。<ref>『現代日本人名録』</ref>1996年定年退任、名誉教授。
2015年6月2日、[[老衰]]のため死去。89歳没。
==著書== *『五行大義』明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1973 *『五行大義の基礎的研究』明徳出版社 1976 *『五行大義校註』汲古書院 1984 *『日本陰陽道書の研究』汲古書院 1985 *『[[菜根譚]]のことば』斯文会 My古典 2007
===共編著=== *『[[緯書]]の基礎的研究』[[安居香山]]共著 漢魏文化研究会 1966 のち国書刊行会 *『原典対照中国思想文学通史』編著 明治書院 1984 *『緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼』編 平河出版社 1993 *『選集道教と日本 第2巻 古代文化の展開と道教』[[野口鉄郎]]共編 雄山閣出版 1997 *『中国人と道教 林原フォーラム’97』編 汲古書院 1998
===訳注など=== *『緯書集成』全6巻 安居香山共編 漢魏文化研究室 1959-64 *『重修緯書集成』全6巻 安居香山共編 明徳出版社 1971-92 *『[[呂氏春秋]]』[[内野熊一郎]]共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1976 *『給食倫理』編著 第一出版 給食学双書 1977 *『[[食経]]』[[佐藤達全]]共編著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1978 *[[道元]]『作る心食べる心 [[典座教訓]]・赴粥飯法・正法眼蔵示庫院文』[[石川力山]]・[[中村信幸]]共訳著 第一出版 1980 『典座教訓・赴粥飯法』講談社学術文庫 1991 *『日本料理秘伝集成 原典現代語訳 第17巻 食品食物学・食医学』佐藤達全共編 同朋舎出版 1985 *『五行大義全釈』[[藤井友子]]共著 明治書院 1986 *[[洪自誠]]『菜根譚』石川力山共訳注 講談社学術文庫 1986 *『[[食物本草]]』佐藤達全共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1987 *[[都良香]]『都氏文集全釈』[[大塚雅司]]共著 汲古書院 1988 *蔡恒息『易のニューサイエンス [[八卦]]・[[太極図]]とコンピュータ』[[武田時昌]]共訳 東方書店 1989 *朱宗元,趙青樹『陰陽五行学説入門』[[中村敞子]]共訳 谷口書店 1990 *『[[周易]]本義』[[古藤友子]]共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書続編 1992 *洪丕謨,姜玉珍『中国算命術』中村敞子共訳 東方書店 1992 *『田氏家集全釈』[[島田伸一郎]]共著 汲古書院 1993 *『五行大義』[[清水浩子]]共著 明治書院 新編漢文選 1998 *『[[風俗通義]]』清水浩子共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書続編 2002 *洪応明『菜根譚 清朝本全訳』訳注 東方書店 2006 *『[[中右記]]部類紙背漢詩集』[[伊野弘子]]共訳注 汲古書院 2011
===記念論集=== *『東洋学論集 中村璋八博士古稀記念』汲古書院 1996

'''中村 璋八'''（なかむら しょうはち、[[1926年]][[1月7日]] - [[2015年]][[6月2日]]<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html 中国哲学者の中村璋八さんが死去：朝日新聞]</ref>）は、中国哲学研究者、[[駒澤大学]]名誉教授。
神奈川県[[愛甲郡]][[愛川町]]生まれ。1951年[[東京文理科大学]]文学科卒。1978年「[[五行大義]]の基礎的研究」で駒澤大学[[文学博士]]。[[東京都立町田高等学校]]教諭、駒澤大学文学部助教授、教授。<ref>『現代日本人名録』</ref>1996年定年退任、名誉教授。
2015年6月2日、[[老衰]]のため死去。89歳没。

'''中村 璋八'''（なかむら しょうはち、[[1926年]][[1月7日]] - [[2015年]][[6月2日]]<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html 中国哲学者の中村璋八さんが死去：朝日新聞]</ref>）は、日本の中国哲学研究者、[[駒澤大学]]名誉教授。
神奈川県[[愛甲郡]][[愛川町]]生まれ。1951年[[東京文理科大学]]文学科卒。1978年「[[五行大義]]の基礎的研究」で駒澤大学[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]。[[東京都立町田高等学校]]教諭、駒澤大学文学部助教授、教授。<ref>『現代日本人名録』</ref>1996年定年退任、名誉教授。
2015年6月2日、[[老衰]]のため死去。89歳没。

{{出典の明記| date = 2023-03}}
'''中村 璋八'''（なかむら しょうはち、[[1926年]][[1月7日]] - [[2015年]][[6月2日]]<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html 中国哲学者の中村璋八さんが死去：朝日新聞]</ref>）は、日本の[[中国哲学]]研究者、[[駒澤大学]]名誉教授。
神奈川県[[愛甲郡]][[愛川町]]生まれ。1951年[[東京文理科大学]]文学科卒。1978年「[[五行大義]]の基礎的研究」で駒澤大学[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]。[[東京都立町田高等学校]]教諭、駒澤大学文学部助教授、教授。<ref>『現代日本人名録』</ref>1996年定年退任、名誉教授。
2015年6月2日、[[老衰]]のため死去。89歳没。

===訳注など=== *『緯書集成』全6巻 安居香山共編 漢魏文化研究室 1959-64 *『重修緯書集成』全6巻 安居香山共編 明徳出版社 1971-92 *『[[呂氏春秋]]』[[内野熊一郎]]共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1976 *『給食倫理』編著 第一出版 給食学双書 1977 *『食経』[[佐藤達全]]共編著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1978 *[[道元]]『作る心食べる心 [[典座教訓]]・赴粥飯法・[[正法眼蔵]]示庫院文』[[石川力山]]・[[中村信幸]]共訳著 第一出版 1980 **新編『典座教訓・赴粥飯法』[[講談社学術文庫]] 1991 *『日本料理秘伝集成 原典現代語訳 第17巻 食品食物学・食医学』佐藤達全共編 同朋舎出版 1985 *『五行大義全釈』藤井友子共著 明治書院 1986 **新編『[[五行大義]] 上・下』[[古藤友子]]・[[清水浩子]]共著 明治書院 新編漢文選 1998 *[[洪自誠]]『菜根譚』石川力山共訳注 講談社学術文庫 1986 *『食物本草』佐藤達全共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1987 *[[都良香]]『都氏文集全釈』大塚雅司共著 汲古書院 1988 *蔡恒息『易のニューサイエンス [[八卦]]・[[太極図]]とコンピュータ』[[武田時昌]]共訳 [[東方書店]] 1989 *朱宗元・趙青樹『[[陰陽五行思想|陰陽五行学説入門]]』中村敞子共訳 谷口書店 1990 *『[[周易]]本義』[[古藤友子]]共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書続編 1992 *洪丕謨、姜玉珍『中国算命術』中村敞子共訳 東方書店 1992 *『田氏家集全釈』[[島田伸一郎]]共著 汲古書院 1993 *『[[風俗通義]]』清水浩子共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書続編 2002 *洪応明『菜根譚 清朝本全訳』訳注 東方書店 2006 *『[[中右記]]部類紙背漢詩集』伊野弘子共訳注 汲古書院 2011
===記念論集=== *『東洋学論集 中村璋八博士古稀記念』汲古書院 1996

revision.comment: Created page with " '''Sangoku Sōden Onmyō Kankatsu Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū''' (三国相伝陰陽輨轄簠簋内伝金烏玉兎集) is a practical and specialized divination text traditionally attributed to Abe no Seimei ({{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}). However, it is believed to have been compiled after Seimei's death, with various theories regarding its date of origin. The work is also referred to as '''Sangoku Sōden Senmyōrekikyōchū''' (三国相伝宣明暦経註)..."
revision.text:
'''Sangoku Sōden Onmyō Kankatsu Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū''' (三国相伝陰陽輨轄簠簋内伝金烏玉兎集) is a practical and specialized divination text traditionally attributed to Abe no Seimei ({{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}). However, it is believed to have been compiled after Seimei's death, with various theories regarding its date of origin.
The work is also referred to as '''Sangoku Sōden Senmyōrekikyōchū''' (三国相伝宣明暦経註), abbreviated as '''Hokinaiden''' (簠簋内伝), '''Hoki''' (簠簋), or '''Kin'u Gyokuto-shū''' (金烏玉兎集).
The term "簠 (Hoki)" and "簋 (Gui)" refer to ancient Chinese ritual vessels.
== Overview == The {{ill|Kin'u|ja|金烏}}, a three-legged golden crow associated with the sun, is a mythical bird symbolizing the sun, while the {{ill|Gyokuto|ja|玉兎}}, a rabbit said to dwell on the moon, represents the moon. These symbols reflect the content of this secret manual of onmyōji, which focuses on understanding the circulation of energy and divination based on the sun and moon's movements.
== Authorship == Although the text is traditionally attributed to Abe no Seimei, it was likely compiled after his death, casting doubt on this claim.
In the mid-Edo period text *Taizan-shū*, Abe no Yasufuku ({{ill|Abe no Yasufuku|ja|土御門泰福}}), the head of the Abe clan's onmyōdō school, is quoted as saying, "Hokinaiden was created by a Shingon monk and is not part of the Abe family tradition. Seimei passed down the astronomy brought back from Tang China by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}."
According to *Nihon Onmyōdō-shi Sōsetsu* (A General Overview of Japanese Onmyōdō), {{ill|Murayama Shūichi|ja|村山修一}} speculates, based on explanations by {{ill|Nishida Osamu|ja|西田長男}}, that the author may have been a shrine priest at Gion Shrine and a descendant of Seimei. After Abe no Yoshihira, Seimei's descendants split into three lines: Toki, Enya, and {{ill|Abe no Yasuchika|ja|安倍泰親}}. It is suggested that a descendant of Enya who joined Gion Shrine, specifically Seichō, might be the author of *Hokinaiden*.
However, {{ill|Nakamura Shōhachi|ja|中村璋八}}, who conducted a comprehensive study of the text, states that there is no definitive evidence supporting Seichō as the author, leaving the authorship unresolved.
== Structure == The text consists of five volumes: * **Volume 1**: Explains the origins of {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and various {{ill|directional deities|ja|方位神}} and their auspicious and inauspicious influences. * **Volume 2**: Details the origins of the primordial deity {{ill|Pangu|ja|盤古}} and his progeny, as well as calendar-based divinations. * **Volume 3**: Discusses additional elements such as {{ill|Nayin|ja|納音}} and {{ill|Kūbō|ja|空亡}}, not covered in the previous volumes. * **Volume 4**: Focuses on {{ill|Feng Shui|ja|風水}} and architectural auspices. * **Volume 5**: Covers {{ill|Esoteric Buddhist astrology|ja|密教占星術}}, specifically {{ill|Shuku-yo|ja|宿曜占術}}.
{{独自研究範囲|Volumes 4 and 5 are markedly different in style from the earlier volumes, suggesting they were later additions.|date=2017年8月}}
== "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" == The text includes a legend titled "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" (天文司郎安部博士吉備后胤清明朝臣入唐伝).<ref>{{NDLDC|1911340/4}}</ref> It recounts the origins of the text, attributing it to knowledge passed from {{ill|Mañjuśrī|ja|文殊菩薩}} in India to Haku-dō Shōnin, then to Abe no Seimei.<ref>*Hokinaiden* manuscripts vary, with some omitting the full narrative.</ref>
== Hokishō == *Hokishō* (簠簋抄) is a commentary on *Hokinaiden* published by the early Edo period. Another earlier commentary, *Hokisōriden* (簠簋袖裡伝), also exists.
== Published Editions == * Fujimaki Ippo, *Abe Seimei "Hokinaiden" Modern Translation and Commentary*, Eibunsha, 2017, ISBN 978-4864032636. * Shimode Tsuneyo (ed.), *Shintō Taikei 103: Onmyōdō*, Shintō Taikei Hensankai.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}
== External Links == * [http://dl.ndl.go.jp/search/searchResult?featureCode=all&searchWord=三國相傳陰陽 National Diet Library listings of *Sangoku Sōden Onmyō Kankatsu Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū*] * [http://codh.rois.ac.jp/pmjt/book/200019239/ *Hokinaiden Kin'u Gyokuto-shū* - Joint Usage Center for Humanities Open Data] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520572357557459072 Religious World of *Hokinaiden*] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1572543026800934912 *Hokinaiden* Notes: Relation to Gozu Tennō Legends]
{{Book-stub}}

{{独自研究範囲|Volumes 4 and 5 are markedly different in style from the earlier volumes, suggesting they were later additions.|date=2017年8月]]
== "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" == The text includes a legend titled "Tenmon Shirō Abe Hakase Kibi Kōin Seimei Chōjin Nittō-den" (天文司郎安部博士吉備后胤清明朝臣入唐伝).<ref>{{NDLDC|1911340/4]]</ref> It recounts the origins of the text, attributing it to knowledge passed from {{ill|Mañjuśrī|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] in India to Haku-dō Shōnin, then to Abe no Seimei.<ref>*Hokinaiden* manuscripts vary, with some omitting the full narrative.</ref>
== Hokishō == *Hokishō* (簠簋抄) is a commentary on *Hokinaiden* published by the early Edo period. Another earlier commentary, *Hokisōriden* (簠簋袖裡伝), also exists.
== Published Editions == * Fujimaki Ippo, *Abe Seimei "Hokinaiden" Modern Translation and Commentary*, Eibunsha, 2017, ISBN 978-4864032636. * Shimode Tsuneyo (ed.), *Shintō Taikei 103: Onmyōdō*, Shintō Taikei Hensankai.
== Notes == {{Reflist]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]]

'''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html {{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died]</ref>) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}.
He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,<ref>『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』</ref> retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor.
He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89.

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Faith|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:祇園信仰]] * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:八坂神社]] {{Source Citation|date=2023-03}}
'''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html {{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died]</ref>) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}.
He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,<ref>『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』</ref> retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor.
He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89.

'''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html {{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died]</ref>) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}.
He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,<ref>『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』</ref> retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor.
He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89.

'''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html {{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died]</ref>) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}.
He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,<ref>『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』</ref> retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor.
He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89.

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Faith|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:祇園信仰]] * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:八坂神社]] {{Source Citation|date=2023-03}}
'''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html {{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died]</ref>) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}.
He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,<ref>『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』</ref> retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor.
He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89.

He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakamura Shōhachi-qq]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.text: [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 ===Co-authored=== * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Weisho|ja|緯書の基礎的研究}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Association 1966, {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}} 1986 * 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu Kōzan|ja|緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼}}』, edited, {{ill|Hirakawa Shuppansha|ja|平河出版社}} 1993 * 『{{ill|Collected Works on Taoism and Japan, Volume 2: The Development of Ancient Culture and Taoism|ja|選集道教と日本 第2巻 古代文化の展開と道教}}』, co-edited with {{ill|Tetsurō Noguchi|ja|野口鉄郎}}, {{ill|Yūzankaku Shuppansha|ja|雄山閣出版}} 1997 * 『{{ill|Chinese People and Taoism: Hayashibara Forum '97|ja|中国人と道教 林原フォーラム’97}}』, edited, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1998 ===Annotations and Translations=== * 『{{ill|Weisho Shūsei|ja|緯書集成}}』, 6 volumes, co-edited with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Laboratory 1959–64 * 『重修{{ill|Weisho Shūsei|ja|緯書集成}}』, 6 volumes, co-edited with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1971–92 * 『{{ill|Lu's Spring and Autumn|ja|呂氏春秋}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Kumaichirō Uchino|ja|内野熊一郎}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1976 * 『{{ill|School Lunch Ethics|ja|給食倫理}}』, edited, Daiichi Shuppan, School [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 ===Co-authored=== * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Weisho|ja|緯書の基礎的研究}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Association 1966, {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}} 1986 * 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 ===Co-authored=== * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Weisho|ja|緯書の基礎的研究}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Association 1966, {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}} 1986 * 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu Kōzan|ja|緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼}}』, edited, {{ill|Hirakawa Shuppansha|ja|平河出版社}} 1993 * 『{{ill|Collected Works on Taoism and Japan, Volume 2: The Development of Ancient Culture and Taoism|ja|選集道教と日本 第2巻 古代文化の展開と道教}}』, co-edited with {{ill|Tetsurō Noguchi|ja|野口鉄郎}}, {{ill|Yūzankaku Shuppansha|ja|雄山閣出版}} 1997 * 『{{ill|Chinese People and Taoism: Hayashibara Forum '97|ja|中国人と道教 林原フォーラム’97}}』, edited, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1998 ===Annotations and Translations=== * 『{{ill|Weisho Shūsei|ja|緯書集成}}』, 6 volumes, co-edited with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Laboratory 1959–64 * 『重修{{ill|Weisho Shūsei|ja|緯書集成}}』, 6 volumes, co-edited with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1971–92 * 『{{ill|Lu's Spring and Autumn|ja|呂氏春秋}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Kumaichirō Uchino|ja|内野熊一郎}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1976 * 『{{ill|School Lunch Ethics|ja|給食倫理}}』, edited, Daiichi Shuppan, School Lunch Studies Series 1977 * 『{{ill|Shokyō|ja|食経}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tatsuo Sato|ja|佐藤達全}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1978 * {{ill|Dōgen|ja|道元}} "The Heart to Create, the Heart to Eat: {{ill|Dianza Kyōkun|ja|典座教訓}}, Fuchokuhanpō, and {{ill|Shōbō Genzō|ja|正法眼蔵}} Documents from Shikoin", co-translated with {{ill|Rikizan Ishikawa|ja|石川力山}} and {{ill|Nakamura Nobuyuki|ja|中村信幸}}, Daiichi Shuppan 1980 ** Revised edition of "Dianza Kyōkun, Fuchokuhanpō" published by {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}} 1991 * 『{{ill|Collected Secrets of Japanese Cuisine: Original Texts in Modern Japanese Translation, Volume 17: Food Studies and Culinary Medicine|ja|日本料理秘伝集成 原典現代語訳 第17巻 食品食物学・食医学}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tatsuo Sato|ja|佐藤達全}}, {{ill|Dōhōsha|ja|同朋舎出版}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Complete Exegesis|ja|五行大義全釈}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tomoko Fujii|ja|藤井友子}}, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1986 ** Revised edition "Gogyō Daigi, Upper and Lower", co-authored with {{ill|Tomoko Furutō|ja|古藤友子}} and {{ill|Hiroko Shimizu|ja|清水浩子}}, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} Revised Selection of Classical Chinese, 1998 * {{ill|Kō Jisei|ja|洪自誠}} "Cai Gen Tan", co-translated and annotated with {{ill|Rikizan Ishikawa|ja|石川力山}}, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}} 1986 * 『{{ill|Shokubutsu Honzō|ja|食物本草}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tatsuo Sato|ja|佐藤達全}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1987 * 『{{ill|Toshi Bunshū Zenshaku|ja|都氏文集全釈}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Masashi Ōtsuka|ja|大塚雅司}}, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1988 * {{ill|Cai Hengxi|ja|蔡恒息}} "New Science of the Yi: The Eight Trigrams, {{ill|Taijitu|ja|太極図}} and Computers", co-translated with {{ill|Tokimasa Takeda|ja|武田時昌}}, {{ill|Tōhō Shoten|ja|東方書店}} 1989 * *{{ill|Zhu Zongyuan|ja|朱宗元}} and {{ill|Zhao Qingshu|ja|趙青樹}} "Introduction to {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Thought|ja|陰陽五行思想}}", co-translated with {{ill|Chōko Nakamura|ja|中村敞子}}, {{ill|Taniguchi Shoten|ja|谷口書店}} 1990 * 『{{ill|Zhouyi Ben'gi|ja|周易本義}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tomoko Furutō|ja|古藤友子}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} (Sequel to {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}}) 1992 * 『{{ill|Chinese Divination Techniques|ja|中国算命術}}』, by {{ill|Hong Pimo|ja|洪丕謨}} and {{ill|Jiang Yuzhen|ja|姜玉珍}}, co-translated with {{ill|Chōko Nakamura|ja|中村敞子}}, {{ill|Tōhō Shoten|ja|東方書店}} 1992 * 『{{ill|Tashi Kachū Zenshaku|ja|田氏家集全釈}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Shinichirō Shimada|ja|島田伸一郎}}, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1993 * 『{{ill|Fūzoku Tōgi|ja|風俗通義}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Hiroko Shimizu|ja|清水浩子}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} (Sequel to {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}}) 2002 * {{ill|Hong Ōmei|ja|洪応明}} "Cai Gen Tan: Complete Translation of the Qing Edition", with translation notes, {{ill|Tōhō Shoten|ja|東方書店}} 2006 * 『{{ill|Complete Exegesis of Yin-Yang Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行全釈}}』, co-translated with {{ill|Chōko Nakamura|ja|中村敞子}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} (Sequel to {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}}) 1992 * 『{{ill|Chū-uki: A Collection of Chinese Poems on Paper from the Back of Documents|ja|中右記部類紙背漢詩集}}』, co-translated and annotated with {{ill|Hiroko Ino|ja|伊野弘子}}, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 20qq ===Memorial Essays=== * 『{{ill|Eastern Studies Collection: In Memory of Dr. Nakamura Shōhachi's 70th Birthday|ja|東洋学論集 中村璋八博士古稀記念}}』, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1996 ==Notes== {{Normdaten}} He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.text: [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 ===Co-authored=== * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Weisho|ja|緯書の基礎的研究}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Association 1966, {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}} 1986 * 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu Kōzan|ja|緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼}}』, edited, {{ill|Hirakawa Shuppansha|ja|平河出版社}} 1993 * 『{{ill|Collected Works on Taoism and Japan, Volume 2: The Development of Ancient Culture and Taoism|ja|選集道教と日本 第2巻 He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.text: [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 ===Co-authored=== * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Weisho|ja|緯書の基礎的研究}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Association 1966, {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}} 1986 * 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu revision.text: [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. revision.text: [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 ===Co-authored=== * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Weisho|ja|緯書の基礎的研究}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Association 1966, {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}} 1986 * 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu Kōzan|ja|緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼}}』, edited, {{ill|Hirakawa Shuppansha|ja|平河出版社}} 1993 * 『{{ill|Collected Works on Taoism and Japan, Volume 2: The Development of Ancient Culture and Taoism|ja|選集道教と日本 第2巻 古代文化の展開と道教}}』, co-edited with {{ill|Tetsurō Noguchi|ja|野口鉄郎}}, {{ill|Yūzankaku Shuppansha|ja|雄山閣出版}} 1997 * 『{{ill|Chinese People and Taoism: Hayashibara Forum '97|ja|中国人と道教 林原フォーラム’97}}』, edited, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1998 ===Annotations and Translations=== * 『{{ill|Weisho Shūsei|ja|緯書集成}}』, 6 volumes, co-edited with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Laboratory 1959–64 * 『重修{{ill|Weisho Shūsei|ja|緯書集成}}』, 6 volumes, co-edited with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1971–92 * 『{{ill|Lu's Spring and Autumn|ja|呂氏春秋}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Kumaichirō Uchino|ja|内野熊一郎}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1976 * 『{{ill|School Lunch Ethics|ja|給食倫理}}』, edited, Daiichi Shuppan, School Lunch Studies Series 1977 * 『{{ill|Shokyō|ja|食経}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tatsuo Sato|ja|佐藤達全}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1978 * {{ill|Dōgen|ja|道元}} "The Heart to Create, the Heart to Eat: {{ill|Dianza Kyōkun|ja|典座教訓}}, Fuchokuhanpō, and {{ill|Shōbō Genzō|ja|正法眼蔵}} Documents from Shikoin", co-translated with {{ill|Rikizan Ishikawa|ja|石川力山}} and {{ill|Nakamura Nobuyuki|ja|中村信幸}}, Daiichi Shuppan 1980 ** Revised edition of "Dianza Kyōkun, Fuchokuhanpō" published by {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}} 1991 * 『{{ill|Collected Secrets of Japanese Cuisine: Original Texts in Modern Japanese Translation, Volume 17: Food Studies and Culinary Medicine|ja|日本料理秘伝集成 原典現代語訳 第17巻 食品食物学・食医学}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tatsuo Sato|ja|佐藤達全}}, {{ill|Dōhōsha|ja|同朋舎出版}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Complete Exegesis|ja|五行大義全釈}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tomoko Fujii|ja|藤井友子}}, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1986 ** Revised edition "Gogyō Daigi, Upper and Lower", co-authored with {{ill|Tomoko Furutō|ja|古藤友子}} and {{ill|Hiroko Shimizu|ja|清水浩子}}, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} Revised Selection of Classical Chinese, 1998 * {{ill|Kō Jisei|ja|洪自誠}} "Cai Gen Tan", co-translated and annotated with {{ill|Rikizan Ishikawa|ja|石川力山}}, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}} 1986 * 『{{ill|Shokubutsu Honzō|ja|食物本草}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tatsuo Sato|ja|佐藤達全}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1987 * 『{{ill|Toshi Bunshū Zenshaku|ja|都氏文集全釈}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Masashi Ōtsuka|ja|大塚雅司}}, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1988 * {{ill|Cai Hengxi|ja|蔡恒息}} "New Science of the Yi: The Eight Trigrams, {{ill|Taijitu|ja|太極図}} and Computers", co-translated with {{ill|Tokimasa Takeda|ja|武田時昌}}, {{ill|Tōhō Shoten|ja|東方書店}} 1989 * *{{ill|Zhu Zongyuan|ja|朱宗元}} and {{ill|Zhao Qingshu|ja|趙青樹}} "Introduction to {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Thought|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory}}", co-translated with {{ill|Chōko Nakamura|ja|中村敞子}}, {{ill|Taniguchi Shoten|ja|谷口書店}} 1990 * 『{{ill|Zhouyi Ben'gi|ja|周易本義}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tomoko Furutō|ja|古藤友子}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} (Sequel to {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}}) 1992 * 『{{ill|Chinese Divination Techniques|ja|中国算命術}}』, by {{ill|Hong Pimo|ja|洪丕謨}} and {{ill|Jiang Yuzhen|ja|姜玉珍}}, co-translated with {{ill|Chōko Nakamura|ja|中村敞子}}, {{ill|Tōhō Shoten|ja|東方書店}} 1992 * 『{{ill|Tashi Kachū Zenshaku|ja|田氏家集全釈}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Shinichirō Shimada|ja|島田伸一郎}}, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1993 * 『{{ill|Fūzoku Tōgi|ja|風俗通義}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Hiroko Shimizu|ja|清水浩子}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} (Sequel to {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}}) 2002 * {{ill|Hong Ōmei|ja|洪応明}} "Cai Gen Tan: Complete Translation of the Qing Edition", with translation notes, {{ill|Tōhō Shoten|ja|東方書店}} 2006 * 『{{ill|Complete Exegesis of Yin-Yang Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行全釈}}』, co-translated with {{ill|Chōko Nakamura|ja|中村敞子}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} (Sequel to {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}}) 1992 * 『{{ill|Chū-uki: A Collection of Chinese Poems on Paper from the Back of Documents|ja|中右記部類紙背漢詩集}}』, co-translated and annotated with {{ill|Hiroko Ino|ja|伊野弘子}}, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 2011 ===Memorial Essays=== * 『{{ill|Eastern Studies Collection: In Memory of Dr. Nakamura Shōhachi's 70th Birthday|ja|東洋学論集 中村璋八博士古稀記念}}』, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu Kōzan|ja|緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼}}』, edited, {{ill|Hirakawa Shuppansha|ja|平河出版社}} 1993 * 『{{ill|Collected Works on Taoism and Japan, Volume 2: The Development of Ancient Culture and Taoism|ja|選集道教と日本 第2巻 古代文化の展開と道教}}』, co-edited with {{ill|Tetsurō Noguchi|ja|野口鉄郎}}, {{ill|Yūzankaku Shuppansha|ja|雄山閣出版}} 1997 * 『{{ill|Chinese People and Taoism: Hayashibara Forum '97|ja|中国人と道教 林原フォーラム’97}}』, edited, He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year }} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year }} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.text: [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:牛頭天王]] [[ja:方位神]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:盤古]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:納音]] [[ja:空亡]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:風水]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:密教占星術]] [[ja:宿曜占術]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakamura Shōhachi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:文殊菩薩]] '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year }} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year }} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New
