revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year }} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year }} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 ===Co-authored=== * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Weisho|ja|緯書の基礎的研究}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Association 1966, {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}} 1986 * 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu Kōzan|ja|緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼}}』, edited, {{ill|Hirakawa Shuppansha|ja|平河出版社}} 1993 * 『{{ill|Collected Works on Taoism and Japan, Volume 2: The Development of Ancient Culture and Taoism|ja|選集道教と日本 第2巻 古代文化の展開と道教}}』, co-edited with {{ill|Tetsurō Noguchi|ja|野口鉄郎}}, {{ill|Yūzankaku Shuppansha|ja|雄山閣出版}} 1997 * 『{{ill|Chinese People and Taoism: Hayashibara Forum '97|ja|中国人と道教 林原フォーラム’97}}』, edited, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1998 ===Annotations and Translations=== * 『{{ill|Weisho Shūsei|ja|緯書集成}}』, 6 volumes, co-edited with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Laboratory 1959–64 * 『重修{{ill|Weisho Shūsei|ja|緯書集成}}』, 6 volumes, co-edited with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1971–92 * 『{{ill|Lu's Spring and Autumn|ja|呂氏春秋}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Kumaichirō Uchino|ja|内野熊一郎}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1976 * 『{{ill|School Lunch Ethics|ja|給食倫理}}』, edited, Daiichi Shuppan, School Lunch Studies Series 1977 * 『{{ill|Shokyō|ja|食経}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tatsuo Sato|ja|佐藤達全}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1978 * {{ill|Dōgen|ja|道元}} "The Heart to Create, the Heart to Eat: {{ill|Dianza Kyōkun|ja|典座教訓}}, Fuchokuhanpō, and {{ill|Shōbō Genzō|ja|正法眼蔵}} Documents from Shikoin", co-translated with {{ill|Rikizan Ishikawa|ja|石川力山}} and {{ill|Nakamura Nobuyuki|ja|中村信幸}}, Daiichi Shuppan 1980 ** Revised edition of "Dianza Kyōkun, Fuchokuhanpō" published by {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}} 1991 * 『{{ill|Collected Secrets of Japanese Cuisine: Original Texts in Modern Japanese Translation, Volume 17: Food Studies and Culinary Medicine|ja|日本料理秘伝集成 原典現代語訳 第17巻 食品食物学・食医学}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tatsuo Sato|ja|佐藤達全}}, {{ill|Dōhōsha|ja|同朋舎出版}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Complete Exegesis|ja|五行大義全釈}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tomoko Fujii|ja|藤井友子}}, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1986 ** Revised edition "Gogyō Daigi, Upper and Lower", co-authored with {{ill|Tomoko Furutō|ja|古藤友子}} and {{ill|Hiroko Shimizu|ja|清水浩子}}, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} Revised Selection of Classical Chinese, 1998 * {{ill|Kō Jisei|ja|洪自誠}} "Cai Gen Tan", co-translated and annotated with {{ill|Rikizan Ishikawa|ja|石川力山}}, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}} 1986 * 『{{ill|Shokubutsu Honzō|ja|食物本草}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tatsuo Sato|ja|佐藤達全}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1987 * 『{{ill|Toshi Bunshū Zenshaku|ja|都氏文集全釈}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Masashi Ōtsuka|ja|大塚雅司}}, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1988 * {{ill|Cai Hengxi|ja|蔡恒息}} "New Science of the Yi: The Eight Trigrams, {{ill|Taijitu|ja|太極図}} and Computers", co-translated with {{ill|Tokimasa Takeda|ja|武田時昌}}, {{ill|Tōhō Shoten|ja|東方書店}} 1989 * *{{ill|Zhu Zongyuan|ja|朱宗元}} and {{ill|Zhao Qingshu|ja|趙青樹}} "Introduction to {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Thought|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory}}", co-translated with {{ill|Chōko Nakamura|ja|中村敞子}}, {{ill|Taniguchi Shoten|ja|谷口書店}} 1990 * 『{{ill|Zhouyi Ben'gi|ja|周易本義}}』, co-authored with '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|12=draft}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|12=draft}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|12=draft}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|12=draft}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|12=draft}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|12=draft}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|12=draft}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=draft}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|12=draft}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=draft}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|12=draft}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=draft}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=draft}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=draft}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=draft}} {{ill|Chinese '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|12=draft}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|12=draft}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|12=draft}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|12=draft}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|12=draft}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|12=draft}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|12=draft}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=draft}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|12=draft}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=draft}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|12=draft}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=draft}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=draft}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=draft}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=draft}} {{ill|Chinese revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|12=draft}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|12=draft}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|12=draft}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|12=draft}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|12=draft}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|12=draft}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|12=draft}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=draft}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|12=draft}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=draft}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|12=draft}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=draft}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=draft}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=draft}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=draft}} {{ill|Chinese revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|12=draft|12=draft}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|12=draft|12=draft}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|12=draft|12=draft}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|12=draft|12=draft}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|12=draft|12=draft}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|12=draft|12=draft}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=draft|12=draft}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=draft|12=draft}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=draft|12=draft}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=draft|12=draft}}』 {{ill|Meitoku He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|12=draft|12=draft}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|12=draft|12=draft}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=draft|12=draft}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=draft|12=draft}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.comment: 25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:中村璋八 up to rev 99457026
revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2023-03}}
'''中村 璋八'''（なかむら しょうはち、[[1926年]][[1月7日]] - [[2015年]][[6月2日]]<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html 中国哲学者の中村璋八さんが死去：朝日新聞]</ref>）は、日本の[[中国哲学]]研究者、[[駒澤大学]]名誉教授。
神奈川県[[愛甲郡]][[愛川町]]生まれ。1951年[[東京文理科大学]]文学科卒。1978年「[[五行大義]]の基礎的研究」で駒澤大学[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]。[[東京都立町田高等学校]]教諭、駒澤大学文学部助教授、教授。<ref>『現代日本人名録』</ref>1996年定年退任、名誉教授。
2015年6月2日、[[老衰]]のため死去。89歳没。
==著書== *『五行大義』[[明徳出版社]] 中国古典新書 1973 *『五行大義の基礎的研究』明徳出版社 1976 *『五行大義校註』[[汲古書院]] 1984 *『日本陰陽道書の研究』汲古書院 1985 *『[[菜根譚]]のことば』斯文会 My古典 2007
===共編著=== *『[[緯書]]の基礎的研究』[[安居香山]]共著 漢魏文化研究会 1966、[[国書刊行会]] 1986 *『原典対照中国思想文学通史』編著 [[明治書院]] 1984 *『緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼』編 平河出版社 1993 *『選集道教と日本 第2巻 古代文化の展開と道教』[[野口鉄郎]]共編 [[雄山閣出版]] 1997 *『中国人と道教 林原フォーラム’97』編 汲古書院 1998
===訳注など=== *『緯書集成』全6巻 安居香山共編 漢魏文化研究室 1959-64 *『重修緯書集成』全6巻 安居香山共編 明徳出版社 1971-92 *『[[呂氏春秋]]』[[内野熊一郎]]共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1976 *『給食倫理』編著 第一出版 給食学双書 1977 *『食経』[[佐藤達全]]共編著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1978 *[[道元]]『作る心食べる心 [[典座教訓]]・赴粥飯法・[[正法眼蔵]]示庫院文』[[石川力山]]・[[中村信幸]]共訳著 第一出版 1980 **新編『典座教訓・赴粥飯法』[[講談社学術文庫]] 1991 *『日本料理秘伝集成 原典現代語訳 第17巻 食品食物学・食医学』佐藤達全共編 同朋舎出版 1985 *『五行大義全釈』藤井友子共著 明治書院 1986 **新編『[[五行大義]] 上・下』[[古藤友子]]・[[清水浩子]]共著 明治書院 新編漢文選 1998 *[[洪自誠]]『菜根譚』石川力山共訳注 講談社学術文庫 1986 *『食物本草』佐藤達全共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書 1987 *[[都良香]]『都氏文集全釈』大塚雅司共著 汲古書院 1988 *蔡恒息『易のニューサイエンス [[八卦]]・[[太極図]]とコンピュータ』[[武田時昌]]共訳 [[東方書店]] 1989 *朱宗元・趙青樹『[[陰陽五行思想|陰陽五行学説入門]]』中村敞子共訳 谷口書店 1990 *『[[周易]]本義』[[古藤友子]]共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書続編 1992 *洪丕謨、姜玉珍『中国算命術』中村敞子共訳 東方書店 1992 *『田氏家集全釈』[[島田伸一郎]]共著 汲古書院 1993 *『[[風俗通義]]』清水浩子共著 明徳出版社 中国古典新書続編 2002 *洪応明『菜根譚 清朝本全訳』訳注 東方書店 2006 *『[[中右記]]部類紙背漢詩集』伊野弘子共訳注 汲古書院 2011
===記念論集=== *『東洋学論集 中村璋八博士古稀記念』汲古書院 1996
== 注 == <references />
{{Normdaten}}

'''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会}} My Classics 2007 ===Co-authored=== * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Weisho|ja|緯書の基礎的研究}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Association 1966, {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}} 1986 * 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu Kōzan|ja|緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼}}』, edited, {{ill|Hirakawa Shuppansha|ja|平河出版社}} 1993 * 『{{ill|Collected Works on Taoism and Japan, Volume 2: The Development of Ancient Culture and Taoism|ja|選集道教と日本 第2巻 古代文化の展開と道教}}』, co-edited with {{ill|Tetsurō Noguchi|ja|野口鉄郎}}, {{ill|Yūzankaku Shuppansha|ja|雄山閣出版}} 1997 * 『{{ill|Chinese People and Taoism: Hayashibara Forum '97|ja|中国人と道教 林原フォーラム’97}}』, edited, {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院}} 1998 ===Annotations and Translations=== * 『{{ill|Weisho Shūsei|ja|緯書集成}}』, 6 volumes, co-edited with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Laboratory 1959–64 * 『重修{{ill|Weisho Shūsei|ja|緯書集成}}』, 6 volumes, co-edited with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} 1971–92 * 『{{ill|Lu's Spring and Autumn|ja|呂氏春秋}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Kumaichirō Uchino|ja|内野熊一郎}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1976 * 『{{ill|School Lunch Ethics|ja|給食倫理}}』, edited, Daiichi Shuppan, School Lunch Studies Series 1977 * 『{{ill|Shokyō|ja|食経}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Tatsuo Sato|ja|佐藤達全}}, {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書}} 1978 * '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|12=draft}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|12=draft}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|12=draft}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|12=draft}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|12=draft}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|12=draft}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|12=draft}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=draft}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|12=draft}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=draft}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|12=draft}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=draft}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=draft}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=draft}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=draft}} {{ill|Chinese '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|qq=draft}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|qq=draft}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|qq=draft}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|qq=draft}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|qq=draft}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|qq=draft}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|qq=draft}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|qq=draft}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|qq=draft}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|qq=draft}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft}} {{ill|Chinese '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|qq=draft}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|qq=draft}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|qq=draft}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|qq=draft}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|qq=draft}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|qq=draft}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|qq=draft}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|qq=draft}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|qq=draft}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|qq=draft}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft}} {{ill|Chinese revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1926|qq=}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 7|qq=}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2015|qq=}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|qq=}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shimbun|qq=}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Philosophy|qq=}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komazawa University|qq=}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikō District|qq=}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa Town|qq=}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Literature|qq=}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|qq=}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi Annotated|qq=}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|qq=}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1985 * 『{{ill|Words of Cai Gen Tan|ja|菜根譚のことば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Words of Cai Gen Tan|qq=}}』 {{ill|Sibunkai|ja|斯文会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sibunkai|qq=}} My Classics 2007 ===Co-authored=== * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Weisho|ja|緯書の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Weisho|qq=}}』, co-authored with {{ill|Angu Kōzan|ja|安居香山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Angu Kōzan|qq=}}, Han-Wei Cultural Research Association 1966, {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=}} 1986 * 『{{ill|A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|ja|原典対照中国思想文学通史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/A Comparative History of Original Texts and Chinese Thought and Literature|qq=}}』, edited, {{ill|Meiji Shoin|ja|明治書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Shoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu Kōzan|ja|緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weigaku Kenkyū revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1926|qq=}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 7|qq=}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2015|qq=}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|qq=}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shimbun|qq=}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Philosophy|qq=}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komazawa University|qq=}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikō District|qq=}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa Town|qq=}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Literature|qq=}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|qq=}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1926|qq=}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 7|qq=}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2015|qq=}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|qq=}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shimbun|qq=}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Philosophy|qq=}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komazawa University|qq=}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikō District|qq=}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa Town|qq=}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Literature|qq=}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|qq=}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi Annotated|qq=}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1926|qq=}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 7|qq=}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2015|qq=}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|qq=}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shimbun|qq=}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Philosophy|qq=}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komazawa University|qq=}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikō District|qq=}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa Town|qq=}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Literature|qq=}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|qq=}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi Annotated|qq=}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|ja|1926年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1926|qq=}} {{ill|January 7|ja|1月7日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 7|qq=}} – {{ill|2015|ja|2015年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2015|qq=}} {{ill|June 2|ja|6月2日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|qq=}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|Asahi Shimbun|ja|朝日新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shimbun|qq=}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese Philosophy|ja|中国哲学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Philosophy|qq=}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|ja|駒澤大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komazawa University|qq=}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District|ja|愛甲郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikō District|qq=}} {{ill|Aikawa Town|ja|愛川町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa Town|qq=}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Literature|ja|博士（文学）|lt=Doctor of Literature|lt_ja=文学博士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctor of Literature|qq=}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|ja|東京都立町田高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|qq=}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi Annotated|qq=}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi Annotated|qq=}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|qq=}}』 Shoin|ja|明治書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Shoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Weigaku Kenkyū Ronsō: In Memoriam Dr. Angu Kōzan|ja|緯学研究論叢 安居香山博士追悼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weigaku Kenkyū '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi Annotated|qq=}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi Annotated|qq=}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|ja|日本陰陽道書の研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Study of Japanese Onmyōdō Documents|qq=}}』 '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi Annotated|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New
