revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi Annotated|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō revision.text: '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō revision.text: {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New Series|ja|中国古典新書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese Classics New Series|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 1973 * 『{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} 1976 * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi Annotated|ja|五行大義校註|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi Annotated|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 {{ill|Kyukokoshoin|ja|汲古書院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyukokoshoin|qq=}} 1984 * 『{{ill|Study of Japanese Onmyōdō revision.text: {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|qid=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|qq=}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|qq=}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|qid=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|qid=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|qid=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|qid=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. revision.text: {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|qid=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|qid=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|qq=|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|1=meitokushuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512068}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|1=meitokushuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512068}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ([[:en:1926|1926]] [[:en:January 7|January 7]] – [[:en:2015|2015]] [[:en:June 2|June 2]][http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html][[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|Asahi Shimbun]]: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese [[:en:Chinese philosophy|Chinese Philosophy]] researcher and Honorary Professor at [[:en:Komazawa University|Komazawa University]]. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in [[:en:Aikō District, Kanagawa|Aikō District]] [[:en:Aikawa, Kanagawa|Aikawa Town]]. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Literature]] from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at [[:en:Machida High School|Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School]], and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|WD=Q2624|lt=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|WD=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|WD=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|WD=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|1=meitokushuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512068}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041882]] Wikidata name: Shouhachi Nakamura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|WD=Q2624|lt=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|WD=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|WD=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|WD=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku
