[[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|WD=Q2624|lt=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|WD=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|WD=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|WD=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|WD=Q2624|lt=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|WD=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|WD=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|WD=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|1=meitokushuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512068}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182}}|native_name=中村璋八}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|WD=Q2624|lt=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|WD=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|WD=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|WD=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|1=meitokushuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512068}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182|1=Philosopher}}|native_name=中村璋八}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7|1=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|WD=Q2624|lt=June 2|1=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|WD=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy|1=Chinese philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|WD=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University|1=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District|1=Aikō District, Kanagawa}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town|1=Aikawa, Kanagawa}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature|1=Doctor of Letters}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|WD=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|1=Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=Senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|1=meitokushuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512068}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182|1=Philosopher}}|native_name=中村璋八}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7|1=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|WD=Q2624|lt=June 2|1=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|WD=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy|1=Chinese philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|WD=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University|1=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District|1=Aikō District, Kanagawa}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town|1=Aikawa, Kanagawa}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature|1=Doctor of Letters}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|WD=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|1=Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=Senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182|1=Philosopher}}|native_name=中村璋八}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7|1=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|WD=Q2624|lt=June 2|1=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|WD=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy|1=Chinese philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|WD=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University|1=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District|1=Aikō District, Kanagawa}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town|1=Aikawa, Kanagawa}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature|1=Doctor of Letters}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|WD=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|1=Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=Senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182|1=Philosopher}}|native_name=中村璋八}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7|1=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|WD=Q2624|lt=June 2|1=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|WD=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy|1=Chinese philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|WD=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University|1=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District|1=Aikō District, Kanagawa}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town|1=Aikawa, Kanagawa}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature|1=Doctor of Letters}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|WD=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|1=Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=Senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|1=meitokushuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512068}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New revision.text: [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|WD=Q4964182|1=Philosopher}}|native_name=中村璋八}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|WD=Q2228|lt=January 7|1=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|WD=Q2624|lt=June 2|1=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|WD=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy|1=Chinese philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|WD=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University|1=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District|1=Aikō District, Kanagawa}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town|1=Aikawa, Kanagawa}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature|1=Doctor of Letters}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|WD=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|1=Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=Senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|1=meitokushuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512068}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] [[ja:中村璋八]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|qid=Q4964182|1=Philosopher}}|native_name=中村璋八}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|qid=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|qid=Q2228|lt=January 7|1=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|qid=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|qid=Q2624|lt=June 2|1=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|qid=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|qid=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy|1=Chinese philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|qid=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University|1=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District|1=Aikō District, Kanagawa}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town|1=Aikawa, Kanagawa}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|qid=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature|1=Doctor of Letters}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|qid=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|1=Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=Senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * revision.text: {{Infobox person|birth_date=1926; {{birth date|1926|1|7}}|death_date={{death date|2015|6|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|philosopher|qid=Q4964182|1=Philosopher}}|native_name=中村璋八}} '''Nakamura Shōhachi''' (中村 璋八) ({{ill|1926|en|1926|qid=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|qid=Q2228|lt=January 7|1=January 7}} – {{ill|2015|en|2015|qid=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}} {{ill|June 2|en|June 2|qid=Q2624|lt=June 2|1=June 2}}[http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASH624K0HH62UCLV009.html]{{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|qid=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}: Chinese philosopher Nakamura Shōhachi has died) is a Japanese {{ill|Chinese philosophy|en|Chinese philosophy|qid=Q184663|lt=Chinese Philosophy|1=Chinese philosophy}} researcher and Honorary Professor at {{ill|Komazawa University|en|Komazawa University|qid=Q910518|lt=Komazawa University|1=Komazawa University}}. He was born in Kanagawa Prefecture, in {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District|1=Aikō District, Kanagawa}} {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa Town|1=Aikawa, Kanagawa}}. He graduated from the Department of Literature at {{ill|Tokyo Bunri University|ja|東京文理科大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bunri University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京文理科大学 (旧制)|1=Tokyo University of Literature and Science|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524885}} in 1951, and in 1978 earned a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|qid=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Literature|1=Doctor of Letters}} from Komazawa University with his work “{{ill|Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義の基礎的研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fundamental Study of Gogyō Daigi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. He served as a teacher at {{ill|Machida High School|en|Machida High School|qid=Q6723565|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Machida High School|1=Machida High School}}, and later as an Associate Professor and Professor in the Faculty of Literature at Komazawa University,『{{ill|Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|現代日本人名録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 retiring in 1996 to become Honorary Professor. He died on June 2, 2015, due to {{ill|old age|ja|老衰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old age|1=Senility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q438971}}, at the age of 89. ==Bibliography== * 『{{ill|Gogyō Daigi|ja|五行大義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō Daigi|1=五行大義|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18863192}}』 {{ill|Meitoku Shuppansha|ja|明徳出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitoku Shuppansha|1=meitokushuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512068}} {{ill|Chinese Classics New
